div1.entryFree:
Intrude, 1) intr. to thrust one's self in without having a right to do so: to i. where I am graced, Tit. II, 1, 27. thou wretched, rash, --ing fool, Hml. III, 4, 31. where's that palace whereinto foul things sometimes i. not? Oth. III, 3, 138.
2) tr. to enter without a right to do so, to invade: why should the worm i. the maiden bud? Lucr. 848.
